content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                322-5901              322-6441              322-6444

December 29, 1977


Mr. Anthony L. Miller
Chief Counsel
Office of the Secretary of State
111 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Miller:

     The Executive Director has asked me to respond to your letter
of December 14, 1977, requesting that the Commission deem certain
amendments to the Conflict of Interest Code of the Office of the
Secretary of State non-substantive within the meaning of 2 Cal.Adm.
Code §18752(b).

     We concur in your conclusion that the changes cited in your
letter are non-substantive in nature. In view of the timing of
this change, we believe the amendments in question should be deemed
made in calendar year 1978. Accordingly, formal amendment of the
Code need not be accomplished until early 1979 and the proposed
amendments shall remain in effect until such time.

     Thank you for writing. If I can be of further assistance in
this or any other matter, please feel free to call on me at 322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:glb

Office of the Secretary of State                    111 Capitol Mall
March Fong Eu                                   Sacramento, California 95814

Executive Office                (916) 445-6371
Certification                   (916) 445-1430
Corporation Index               (916) 445-2900
Corporation Records             (916) 445-1768
Election Division               (916) 445-0820
Legal Division (Corp.)          (916) 445-0620
Notary Public Division          (916) 445-6507
State Archives                  (916) 445-4293
Uniform Commercial Code (916) 445-8061


December 14, 1977


Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Proposed Amendments to Agency Conflict of
Interest Code and Request for Approval
to Classify Amendment "Nonsubstantive"
(Cal. Admin. Code, tit. 2, § 18752)

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Pursuant to section 18752, subdivision (b), of the
California Administrative Code, the Secretary of State
proposes to amend section 20127 of the Conflict of
Interest Code of the Office of the Secretary of State
(Cal. Admin. Code, tit. 2, div. 7, ch. 2) to add
"Corporation Assistant" and "Legislative Coordinator"
as "designated employees" and to include these
positions within disclosure categories II and I
respectively. I should appreciate your submitting
such proposed changes to the commission for review
and approval.

These positions are newly created and will be included
in existing disclosure categories. The Secretary of
State believes such amendments are nonsubstantive
within the meaning of California Administrative Code
section 18752, subdivisions (d)(4), and requests
written approval to so classify the amendments pursuant
to California Administrative Code section 18752, sub-
division (b).

Mr. Michael Bennett                 2                 December 14, 1977


For your information, I am sending descriptions of the
newly created positions.

                                                     Sincerely,

                                                     [signature]
                                                     ANTHONY L. MILLER
                                                     Chief Counsel

ALM:gp
Enc.
